summary: This cluster of research focuses on the isolation and characterization of novel alkaliphilic microalgae from Lake Okeechobee, Florida, specifically the strains *Chloroidium sp. 154-1*, *Chlorella sp. 154-2*, and *Fustulifera sp. 154-3*, for biofuel applications. A key finding across the studies was the high lipid accumulation in *Fustulifera sp. 154-3*, reaching 20-30% of dry weight, with a fatty acid profile dominated by palmitoleic acid (52%) and palmitic acid (17%), alongside a significant proportion of eicosapentaenoic acid (15%). The work underscores the potential of these alkaliphilic diatoms as feedstocks for sustainable biofuel production, linking specific fatty acid compositions to biomass quality.
